metadata.title: X29 - Full-Stack & Cross-Platform Engineer
metadata.description: X29 is a full-stack engineer working across web, desktop, and mobile with React, NestJS, Electron, React Native, and Taro.

hero.intro: From the database to the pixel, web to desktop to mobile. I take ownership of the whole stack and care about how the product actually feels in someone's hands.

about.label: Profile
about.heading: I own products end to end, from API design to the last frame of an animation.
about.paragraphOne: Six years building real products: React and TypeScript on the front, NestJS and Node on the back, Electron for desktop, React Native and Taro for mobile and mini-programs. One person, the whole pipeline.
about.paragraphTwo: I think like an owner, not a ticket-taker. I would rather understand why something is being built and push back when it is wrong than ship a feature nobody needed. Performance, motion, and the details are where I do my best work.

items.name: StoreForge
items.detail: StoreForge is a low-code platform for building e-commerce storefronts, architected as a pnpm + Turborepo monorepo. A visual editor (React + dnd-kit) produces a PageSchema; a NestJS backend validates and versions it with Prisma + PostgreSQL; a Next.js storefront consumes it and renders with a shared isomorphic block library, so what you edit in the admin is exactly what ships. I designed the PageSchema contract, the editor's command-based undo/redo store, the iframe-sandboxed canvas, and the multi-tenant data isolation enforced at the ORM layer.

items.name: EZ Remote
items.detail: A desktop client for P2P remote control, file transfer, terminal, and port forwarding, built on Electron with a three-process architecture. A C++ SDK runs in an isolated utilityProcess so a crash auto-restarts in 200ms without taking down the app; the main process talks to it over a custom RPC with timeouts. I built the session model where one P2P connection multiplexes desktop, SFTP, terminal (node-pty) and port-forward channels, an SSH2-over-SDK tunnel for file transfer, and an application-level protocol on the SDK's binary channel.

items.name: Rewind
items.detail: Rewind is an open-source monitoring platform that makes production errors traceable: session recording and replay, error-fingerprint grouping with >95% dedup accuracy, SourceMap stack restoration, blank-screen detection, and AI-assisted context summaries. Monorepo with a zero-dependency collection SDK (gzip < 15KB), a NestJS + PostgreSQL + Redis + BullMQ backend, and a React + Vite dashboard. I work across the SDK collection engine, the data pipeline, and the localization workbench UI.

items.name: Wanderlust
items.summary: Travel-guide social app for visitors to China
items.detail: An Instagram-style mobile community where Japanese travelers share guides and itineraries for visiting China, built with React Native, Expo, and TypeScript. I owned the app development: a performant image-heavy feed with infinite scroll, the post composer and media viewer, gesture-driven navigation, and an i18n layer for Japanese and Chinese. Shipped as a cross-platform iOS/Android codebase.

contact.label: Contact
contact.heading: Let's build something good
contact.intro: If you are hiring, collaborating, or just curious about the code behind this page, my inbox is open. I reply to everything that is real.
